text.title: SEO and AEO Mastery Overview
text.summary: Dual optimization strategy for traditional search engines and AI assistants, with the E-E-A-T framework, toolset, and agent selection guidance.

rule.statement: Optimize content for both traditional search engines (Google, Bing) AND AI assistants (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude) simultaneously.
rule.rationale: Both paths require clear structure, authoritative content, proper markup, and fast loading. The same technical quality that satisfies Google also enables AI crawlers to cite the content.

list.item: Technical: robots.txt, sitemap.xml, crawl budget, Core Web Vitals
On-Page: semantic HTML, schema markup, headings, meta tags
Multilingual: hreflang, URL structure, x-default
AI/AEO: llms.txt, structured data, entity optimization
Social: Open Graph, Twitter Cards, LinkedIn
Accessibility: WCAG 2.2, ARIA, screen readers, semantic markup
Performance: LCP < 2.5s, INP < 200ms, CLS < 0.1

rule.statement: Signal Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, and Trustworthiness in content and site structure.
rule.rationale: Google uses E-E-A-T as a quality evaluator. Missing any dimension — particularly Trustworthiness (HTTPS, contact info, privacy policy) — reduces ranking potential regardless of content quality.

list.item: Experience: first-hand knowledge, case studies, original data
Expertise: author credentials, depth of content, citations
Authoritativeness: backlinks, brand mentions, industry recognition
Trustworthiness: HTTPS, contact info, privacy policy, no deceptive patterns
Google Search Console — indexing, Core Web Vitals, coverage errors
PageSpeed Insights — performance analysis and CWV scoring
Rich Results Test — schema markup validation
Ahrefs / Semrush — backlinks, keywords, competitive audits
Screaming Frog — technical crawling and link audit
WAVE — accessibility testing
Keyword research and data extraction: haiku — list compilation, lookup tasks
Analyzing SEO metrics and patterns: sonnet — comparative analysis
Developing SEO strategy and market positioning: opus — complex strategy formulation
